description: The Applied Mathematics-Economics concentration is designed to reflect the mathematical and statistical nature of modern economic theory and empirical research. If you are interested in declaring a concentration in Applied Mathematics-Economics, please refer tothis pagefor more information regarding the process. Students can also use APMA 1650 but must also complete the APMA 1655 online bridgework course, pass the in-person bridgework exam that is offered once per semester, and have taken multivariable calculus and linear algebra

tracks.name: Standard program for the A.B. degree (Mathematical Finance track):

groups.name: Prerequisites:

courses.name: Linear Algebra and Multivariable Calculus for Applied Mathematicians
Linear Algebra and Multivariable Calculus for Applied Mathematicians

groups.name: Course Requirements: 13 Courses: 6 Applied Math and 7 Economics
Applied Mathematics Requirements

courses.name: Applied Ordinary Differential Equations with Theory

groups.name: and
APMA 0365 Applied Partial Differential Equations I with Theory
Select one of the following:

courses.name: Introduction to Object-Oriented Programming and Computer Science
Introduction to Probability and Statistics with Theory1

groups.name: Select one of the following:

courses.name: Introduction to Numerical Solution of Differential Equations
Monte Carlo Simulation with Applications to Finance (preferred)
Recent Applications of Probability and Statistics

groups.name: APMA 193X, 194X Senior Seminar series, depending on topic
Economics Requirements:
Select two 1000-level courses from the "financial economics" group:

courses.name: Venture Capital, Private Equity, and Entrepreneurship

groups.name: Select one 1000-level course from the "mathematical economics" group:

courses.name: Welfare Economics and Social Choice Theory
Advanced Macroeconomics: Monetary, Fiscal, and Stabilization Policies
Topics in Macroeconomics, Development and International Economics
Machine Learning, Text Analysis, and Economics
Game Theory and Applications to Economics

groups.name: Select one 1000-level course from the "data methods" group:

courses.name: Inequality of Opportunity in the US
Health, Hunger and the Household in Developing Countries
Machine Learning, Text Analysis, and Economics
